goal: Stop the selection board loading the full-size 00-back; use the 200px thumbnail
done: getCardBackUrl(data.js) now takes {thumb}: thumb:true returns 00-back-200.webp (~12-20KB) instead of the full 00-back.webp (99-410KB). main.js applyCardBackBackground takes {thumb} and the board call sites (staticCardBacks + per-tile cardBack) pass thumb:true. Reading-flip + card-image fallback paths keep full-res (no thumb).
root_cause: The 12-tile selection board rendered the full 00-back (163KB moonmallow, up to 410KB veila) at ~40-64px, blocking first board paint, while the matching 00-back-200 thumbnails sat unused on the CDN.
fix: Board backs use 00-back-200 (12-24x lighter first paint). Verified live (chrome-devtools): all 12 board tiles load 00-back-200.webp.
verified: Browser: question-draw board renders 12 backs all from 00-back-200.webp; reading-flip path still full 00-back.

goal: Wire the homepage 'Your decks' strip to real decks (it was redesign placeholder markup)
done: main.js renderHomeDeckStrip() populates .home-deck-strip__row from getAllDecks(): each tile shows the deck's real 00-back-200 thumbnail (graceful fallback to full 00-back via applyImageFallback), real EN + TH name, is-locked dim + lock badge for un-unlockable decks (canUnlockDeck), Active badge for the current deck. Tiles link to profile.html (display-only; switching stays on the decks page, so no deck-switch repaint requirement). Imports getAllDecks/getActiveDeckId/canUnlockDeck.
root_cause: The 'Your decks' strip in index.html was static redesign-stub markup: gradient + 'M' monogram tiles with fake names (Velvet Familiar/Honey Hour), never wired to deck data, so no deck back ever showed.
fix: JS-render real deck tiles with their 00-back-200 thumbnails + names + lock/active state.
verified: Browser (chrome-devtools): 11 real tiles, 0 monograms; moonmallow Active+full-color, locked decks dimmed+lock; thumbnails load from 00-back-200.webp; screenshot confirmed.
notes: EN homepage only; th/index.html is the separate off-limits old layout (Phase-7 deferred) with no such strip, so no parity gap introduced.
